channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta tests eBay listings in Facebook Marketplace to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025
item.description: AI in action: How agencies are building out trusted artificial intelligence capabilities The post Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Sam Altman’s sister accuses OpenAI CEO of sexually abusing her
item.description: She accused the OpenAI chief executive officer of sexually abusing her for almost a decade, in a lawsuit filed in federal court.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Bluesky Surpasses Truth Social in User Share
item.description: A left-leaning cohort of social users flocking to Bluesky has made the platform more popular than far-right alternative Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DAMAC Founder to Invest $20 Billion in US Data Center Infrastructure | News Article
item.description: US president-elect Donald Trump confirms plans to invest $20 billion into US data center infrastructure, with Emirati billionaire Hussain Sajwani providing the funding.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New research shows a quarter of freshwater animals are threatened with extinction
item.description: New research shows about a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Getting AI right in 2025: control, control, control
item.description: Businesses face challenges with AI adoption; focusing on control, data privacy, and specialized tools ensures success.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Disney Plus is working on its second reimagining of the cult '90s children’s book Holes – and this one will feature a female lead
item.description: Disney Plus has ordered a pilot for a reboot of the '90s novel 'Holes', but this time a female lead will take center stage.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: This Nintendo Switch 2 mockup from CES 2025 is allegedly based on the console itself, making us think an official announcement has to be imminent
item.description: Accessory maker Genki shows off Nintendo Switch 2 mockup at CES 2025 - and apparently it's very close to the real thing.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 6 fast ways to free up space on your Windows PC
item.description: Want to free up space on your computer? With the right tools, you can quickly eliminate gigabytes of unnecessary files and get back to work — or whatever else you use your computer for. I’ve long argued you don’t need a “PC cleaner” app. You mostly just need the tools built right into Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Website certificates that expire every six weeks? What IT should know
item.description: Industry forces — led by Apple and Google — are pushing for a sharp acceleration of how often website certificates must be updated, but the stated security reason is raising an awful lot of eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 6 swift steps for a faster Android experience
item.description: Well, I’ll be: It’s a new year! Already. Somehow. I think. (For full disclosure, I’m still at least 77% asleep from my traditional end-of-year hibernation/hiatus. Kindly forgive any mid-sentence snores or nonsensical outbursts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CIO y CCO: la importancia de la comunicación en proyectos TI
item.description: Una de las claves para garantizar una buena implementación de cualquier iniciativa tecnológica no tiene nada que ver con la propia tecnología: es la comunicación.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Point2 aims to cut data center power consumption through smart cabling
item.description: AI applications and ML workloads account for nearly 20% of data center electricity consumption today, according to the Electric Power Research Institute (EPRI), and data centers are projected to represent up to 9.1% of total U.S. electricity generation annually by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biggest Nvidia takeaways from Jensen Huang's CES 2025 keynote
item.description: Nvidia’s stock is on the upswing after its CEO, Jensen Huang, unveiled a suite of new products, services and partnerships at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Facebook to end fact-checking, boost political content
item.description: The company said automated moderation would focus on serious issues like terrorism and child sexual exploitation instead.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ally Dana White among Meta’s 3 new directors
item.description: Tapping White to join the board could be seen as Mark Zuckerberg’s latest maneuver to improve ties with Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cybertruck tracking in Las Vegas case raises privacy concerns
item.description: Elon Musk commented on the explosion shortly after it happened, saying it was not an issue with the Cybertruck itself.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Planes, trains and third-party risks
item.description: Christmas Eve (and Christmas Day) are arguably the most important time-frame for transportation companies. So it was a big deal when an American Airlines system glitch forced the airline to ask the government for a full shutdown on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI PCs
item.description: Virtually every aspect of technology has been affected, and potentially improved, by artificial intelligence (AI). PCs are no exception. While the meaning of “AI PC” is still evolving, it generally refers to a computer specifically designed to excel at AI-powered tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: For Apple IT admins, the new year means it’s a good time to upskill
item.description: Continuing education has always been important for IT professionals, but the need to keep your skill set up to date is now more important than ever.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI, power availability and Intel’s future
item.description: As 2025 kicks off, it’s time to dust off the crystal ball. Here are my data center predictions for the coming year. 1) Trump backs off on tech tariffs For all the handwringing, Trump has a history of not following through on his threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fine-tuning Azure OpenAI models in Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) like the OpenAI models used by Azure are general-purpose tools for building many different types of generative AI-powered applications, from chatbots to agent-powered workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World
